label: Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning

node.label: Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning
node.definition: Intermediate computation is expanded in tokens, hidden states, or recurrent steps before committing to an answer.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning is modeled as depending on Decoder-only causal transformers, Autoregressive decoding and feeding downstream nodes such as Self-consistency & best-of-N inference, Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, ReAct-style reasoning and acting.
examples: atlas downstream link to Self-consistency & best-of-N inference
atlas downstream link to Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning
atlas downstream link to ReAct-style reasoning and acting
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Decoder-only causal transformers, Autoregressive decoding.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning has a `uses` dependency on Decoder-only causal transformers because Decoder-only causal transformers is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning has a `uses` dependency on Autoregressive decoding because Autoregressive decoding is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning has a `uses` dependency on In-context & test-time learning because In-context & test-time learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
